footer.builtWithLove: Built with love to help you simplify your life.

home.metaTitle: How to Declutter Your Home | DeclutterSpace
home.metaDescription: Learn how to declutter your home with our step-by-step guide. Get practical tips to declutter room-by-room and build habits that keep your space organized.
home.heroSubtitle: What you keep shapes how you live. When you take the time to revisit your belongings, you're not just organizing objects — you're redefining what deserves space in your life.
home.quoteTitle: It's about consciously keeping items that genuinely enhance your life.
home.thinkingQuote1: "Do I really need this? Or is it time to let go?"
home.unusedGadgetsDesc: It seemed so useful when I bought it, but it hasn't been touched in years. Is it time to reclaim that space?
home.forgottenClothesDesc: They still fit and look good, but I haven't worn them in ages. Are they worth keeping, or should I declutter my closet and donate them?
home.stackOfBooksDesc: They inspire me, but they've been collecting dust. Should I focus on a select few and donate the rest?
home.deadlineQuote: "Set a Declutter Deadline: Use It or Let It Go"
home.unusedGadgetsDeadline: Give yourself one month to use it. If it doesn't prove its value, it's time to declutter.
home.forgottenClothesDeadline: Plan an occasion to wear them within three months. If they stay in the closet, it's time to declutter and donate.
home.stackOfBooksDeadline: Select one book to finish in two months. If it doesn't captivate you, declutter your shelf and pass it along to someone else.
home.feature1Desc: Adding your items in bulk by uploading photos of them, this app for decluttering uses AI to help you easily identify and list belongings.
home.feature2Desc: Assign deadlines to declutter specific items. We'll remind you when it's time to decide.
home.feature3Desc: Monitor your decluttering journey with progress reports. As the leading app for decluttering, we help you celebrate your steps toward a clutter-free home.
home.howToTitle: How to Declutter Your Home in 5 Simple Steps
home.howToSubtitle: Learning how to declutter your home doesn't have to be overwhelming. Follow these five steps to transform your space systematically and create a home that supports the life you want to live.
home.step1Desc: Before you touch anything, decide what kind of home you want. How to declutter your home starts in your mind, not with your hands. Visualize the peaceful, functional space you deserve.
home.step2Title: Tackle One Room at a Time
home.step2Desc: When you declutter your home room by room, you see real progress and stay motivated. Start small—begin with a single closet or one corner of a room. Master that space before moving on.
home.step3Desc: Create keep, donate/sell, and discard piles. Be honest with yourself—when you declutter your home, ask: "Does this item serve a purpose or bring me joy?" If not, it goes.
home.step4Desc: Give yourself a timeframe to decide. When you declutter your home with deadlines, you avoid decision fatigue. Use our app to set reminders and track when it's time to revisit unsure items.
home.step5Desc: The final step in how to declutter your home is keeping it that way. Adopt a one-in, one-out rule: for every new item that enters your home, let one leave. Regularly review your belongings to prevent clutter from rebuilding.
home.roomByRoomTitle: How to Declutter Your Home Room by Room
home.roomByRoomSubtitle: The most effective way to declutter your home is to focus on one room at a time. Here's how to approach each major area of your home for maximum impact.
home.livingRoomDesc: Remove excess throw pillows, organize media cabinets, and clear surfaces. When you declutter your living room, focus on creating a relaxing space that invites conversation and rest.
home.bedroomDesc: Declutter your bedroom by clearing under the bed, limiting clothing to what you actually wear, and removing items that don't belong in a restful space. Your bedroom should be a sanctuary.
home.kitchenDesc: When you declutter your kitchen, start with cabinets and drawers you rarely open. Donate duplicates (two can openers?) and toss expired spices. A organized kitchen makes cooking a joy.
home.bathroomDesc: Dispose of expired medications and products. Declutter your bathroom by keeping only what you use daily. Limit backup toiletries to a small bin—you can always restock when needed.
home.homeOfficeDesc: Paper clutter piles up fast. To declutter your home office, sort papers into action, reference, and recycle. Organize cables and donate old electronics you no longer use.
home.closetsDesc: When you declutter your closets, use the "hang it if you wore it" rule—if you didn't wear it in the past year, donate it. Seasonal rotation helps maintain closet clarity year-round.
home.faq1Title: How is this different from other decluttering apps?
home.faq1Desc: Unlike other solutions that just teach you how to declutter your home, our app for decluttering focuses specifically on helping you make decisions about items you're unsure about. Instead of just organizing everything, we help you determine what truly deserves space in your life through our unique deadline-based approach.
home.faq2Title: Will this really help me declutter?
home.faq2Desc: Yes! We don't just show you how to declutter your home - we combine psychological insights with practical tools. By setting concrete deadlines and tracking your usage of items, you'll make more informed decisions about what to keep or let go.
home.faq3Title: Why I didn't receive notification emails when my items are due?
home.faq3Desc: It might be because your email provider mistakenly spammed our email. Please look for them in the spam folder. If problem persists, please contact us for further assistance.
home.heroImageAlt: Illustration of a decluttered minimalist space

homeCtas.dialogSpentTime: Spend some time on this. Do what you can. Set a timer if you want.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: Have questions or feedback? We'd love to hear from you. Reach out to us anytime.
contact.alreadySubmitted: You have already submitted the form

dashboard.secondLookTitle: You can take a second look later, we will remind you via email.
dashboard.secondLookSubtitle: If by the time you receive the email reminder, and you still haven't use it or it hasn't brought value into your life, maybe it is time to let go

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At DeclutterSpace, we are committed to protecting your privacy and ensuring the security of your personal information. This Privacy Policy outlines how we collect, use, and safeguard your data when you use our web app and services.
privacy.accountInfo: Account Information: When you sign up, we collect your name, email address, and password.
privacy.uploadedContent: Uploaded Content: Photos or details of your items shared through the app.
privacy.paymentInfo: Payment Information: If you purchase a subscription or service, we collect payment details (processed securely by third-party providers).
privacy.usageData: Usage Data: Information about how you interact with our app, such as pages visited and features used.
privacy.deviceData: Device Data: Information about the device you use, including IP address, browser type, and operating system.
privacy.toProvide: To Provide Services: To operate and improve our app, process transactions, and manage your account.
privacy.toCommunicate: To Communicate: To send updates, respond to inquiries, and provide customer support.
privacy.toImprove: To Improve Our Services: To analyze usage data and enhance app functionality.
privacy.toSecurity: To Ensure Security: To detect and prevent fraudulent or unauthorized activities.
privacy.sharingIntro: We do not sell or rent your personal information. However, we may share your data with:
privacy.serviceProviders: Service Providers: Trusted third parties that assist with app functionality, payment processing, and customer support.
privacy.legalObligations: Legal Obligations: When required by law or to protect the rights and safety of our users.
privacy.dataRetentionDesc: We retain your data for as long as necessary to provide our services and comply with legal obligations. You can request deletion of your data by contacting us at: <email>hi@declutterspace.net</email>.
privacy.right1: Access, update, or delete your personal information.
privacy.right3: Request a copy of your data in a portable format.
privacy.yourRightsContact: To exercise these rights, contact us at: <email>hi@declutterspace.net</email>.
privacy.securityDesc: We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your data. However, no method of transmission or storage is 100% secure. Please use our services at your discretion.
privacy.changesDesc: We may update this policy periodically. Changes will be effective immediately upon posting. Please review this policy regularly to stay informed.
privacy.contactUsDesc: For questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at: <email>hi@declutterspace.net</email>.

customerService.title: Customer Service Policy
customerService.intro: At DeclutterSpace, we value your satisfaction and strive to provide a seamless and enjoyable experience. This Refund and Dispute Policy outlines the process for refunds and resolving disputes related to our services.
customerService.refund1: Refunds are available for subscription-based services if requested within 14 days of the original purchase, provided you have not extensively used the service.
customerService.refund2: If a technical issue occurs and we are unable to resolve it within a reasonable timeframe, you may be eligible for a refund.
customerService.refund3: Refunds are not available for services already rendered or completed.
customerService.nonRefundableDesc: Certain purchases, such as one-time downloadable content or special promotions, are non-refundable. These items will be clearly marked at the time of purchase.
customerService.process1: Submit your refund request by emailing us at <email>hi@declutterspace.net</email> with the subject line "Refund Request."
customerService.process2: Include your name, email address, and proof of purchase in the email body.
customerService.process3: Our team will review your request and respond within 5-7 business days.
customerService.disputeIntro: If you are dissatisfied with our service, we encourage you to contact us to resolve the issue before filing a dispute. We are committed to finding a solution that works for you.
customerService.dispute1: Contact our support team at <email>hi@declutterspace.net</email> and provide details of the issue.
customerService.dispute2: If the issue cannot be resolved, you may escalate it to a formal dispute through your payment provider.
customerService.contactUsDesc: For any questions or concerns regarding this Refund and Dispute Policy, please email us at <email>hi@declutterspace.net</email>. We're here to help!

verifyEmail.pleaseWait: Please wait while we verify your email address.

payment.successMessage: Thank you for your purchase. Let's get started
payment.failedMessage: Something went wrong with your payment. Please try again. If the problem persists, please contact hi@declutterspace.net.

checklist.metaDescription: Free declutter checklist to track daily tasks. Organize room-by-room, set deadlines, and build decluttering habits that stick.
checklist.headerTitle: What you keep shapes how you live
checklist.momentumDesc: Decluttering is an ongoing progress, make sure to check back and build momentum.
checklist.ongoingDesc: Your declutter checklist resets each day, while your daily progress stays part of the bigger picture.
checklist.tryTasks: try to actually do 1 or 2 tasks today, every bit counts.
checklist.tapBar: Tap a highlighted bar to inspect that day
checklist.dailyProgressDesc: These are the items you completed on that day.
checklist.reminderQuote: "When you revisit your belongings, you're deciding what deserves space in your life."
checklist.reminderDesc: Your checklist resets each day, but your consistency keeps compounding.
checklist.confirmRemoveTitle: Pew, done with this for good? Press yes to confirm.
checklist.confirmRemoveDesc: This item will still be kept in your decluttering progress.
checklist.cloudTiedToAccount: Your checklist is tied to your account.
checklist.cloudSyncing: Saving your latest checklist changes to the cloud now.
checklist.cloudError: Cloud sync hit a problem. Your local browser copy is still kept on this device.
checklist.cloudSaved: Your lists and progress are stored in the cloud and stay available after you sign back in.
checklist.loginCloudTitle: Log in to keep your checklist secure in the cloud.
checklist.loginCloudDesc: Without login, your lists and progress stay only in this browser. Sign in with Google to back them up to your account.
checklist.journeyNotStarted: Your journey day count will start as soon as you complete your first checklist item.
checklist.focusAreaDesc: {count} recorded clears, which is {percentage}% of all tracked progress so far.
checklist.focusAreaEmpty: Once you have history, this card will highlight the area where you declutter most often.
checklist.fullChartDesc: Move backward or forward in 30-day steps to review the days you showed up.
checklist.mainAreasDesc: The spaces where you tend to make the most progress.
checklist.noProgressYet: No recorded progress yet. Complete a few checklist items and your main areas will show up here.
checklist.topItemsDesc: These are the items that appear most often across your check-ins.
checklist.topItemsEmpty: Your top repeated items will appear here once you build some history.
checklist.uploadHeaderTitle: Bring your own declutter lists in one CSV
checklist.csvFormatDesc: Use one CSV file with two columns: one for the list title, one for the item. One file can cover as many lists as you want.
checklist.importTitle: Import your lists in one step
checklist.crossedItemsKept: Crossed items will always be kept and merged into the new active lists.
checklist.mergeReplaceDesc: Merge adds the CSV into your current active lists. Replace swaps your current active lists for the new import.
checklist.previewDesc: Review the titles and items before saving them into your checklist.
checklist.uploadToPreview: Upload a CSV to preview your lists here.
checklist.uploadReplaceSuccess: Replaced your current lists from {file}. Previously crossed items were kept and merged with the new import.
checklist.uploadMergeSuccess: Merged {count} lists from {file} into your current lists.
